diagrams.description: Session events are partitioned into exclusive attribution classes; mixed parents degrade audit readiness without reallocating tokens or blocking development.
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  J[Session events] --> C[Deterministic cue classifier]
  C --> S[Strict story]
  C --> W[Worktree associated]
  C --> O[Other story]
  C --> U[Unclassified]
  S --> R[Attribution risk]
  W --> R
  O --> R
  R --> A[Audit readiness]

clauses.statement: A synthetic session containing two story cue sets reports mixed_parent, detected story ids, and strict-to-associated divergence.
A single-story session reports mixed_parent false without changing existing token or semantic bucket totals.
Every event belongs to exactly one of strict, associated-only, other-story, or unclassified and their counts sum to the target event count.
Attribution risk becomes high only below the declared strict-to-associated threshold and mixed parents make audit readiness partial.
PR preparation stores a non-blocking session_boundary note and delegates mixed-session determination to audit session-cost.
When no session can be selected, attribution is explicitly unavailable with a reason instead of being omitted.

Unreadable selected JSONL files make attribution explicitly unavailable with a bounded reason. Malformed rows remain unclassified while valid rows stay available only with partial parse coverage and a session_attribution_partial_parse readiness blocker.

test_refs.case: preserves valid rows and accounts malformed JSONL rows

test_pattern.must_cover: fails attribution closed when a selected JSONL file cannot be read
preserves valid rows and accounts malformed JSONL as unattributed

clauses.statement: Explicit non-auto selection with no resolved session preserves the selection reason and now returns an unavailable attribution projection instead of omitting it.

test_refs.case: keeps unavailable attribution explicit when inference selects no session

test_pattern.must_cover: keeps unavailable attribution explicit when inference selects no session

clauses.statement: Requested non-auto session inference behavior remains unchanged.
Session metadata continues to establish the canonical session id and cwd while JSONL input is parsed.
